newSkills.name: skill-security-scan
newSkills.description: Security checks for installing skills, packages, or plugins. Run before any install commands to verify source, dependencies, and detect malicious patterns.
newSkills.notes: Essential safety tool. Checks npm packages, detects lifecycle scripts, verifies sources, scans for dynamic content fetching. Highly recommended before any skill install.

newSkills.name: claw-calendar-new-skill
newSkills.description: 智能日历助手：通过 REST API 与 Claw Calendar 交互，管理日历和事件，支持 .ics 订阅同步到手机。
newSkills.notes: Useful for calendar management. Requires CLAW_CALENDAR_API_KEY. Clean implementation with environment variable config. No suspicious behavior.

newSkills.name: skill-engineer
newSkills.description: Multi-agent skill development workflow - orchestrates Designer, Reviewer, and Tester subagents for quality-gated skill development.
newSkills.notes: Powerful for building/reviewing skills. Uses multi-agent architecture for design, review, testing. Good for skill quality assurance.

newSkills.name: skill-from-memory
newSkills.description: Extract workflows, solutions, and patterns from conversation history or memory files, package them as skills, and publish to GitHub/ClawHub.
newSkills.notes: Interesting for automating skill creation from past work. Requires careful review of extracted content before publishing. Security audit: warns about private data exposure risk.

newSkills.name: universal-skills-manager
newSkills.description: Discovers skills from SkillsMP, SkillHub, and ClawHub, manages installation across Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and other AI tools.
newSkills.notes: Cross-platform skill management. Useful for aggregating skills from multiple sources. API-dependent (SkillsMP requires key).

summary: Found 5 notable skills. skill-security-scan is essential for safe skill installation. skill-engineer is valuable for skill development workflows. Others are moderate utility depending on use case.
searchNotes: ClawHub search didn't return specifically new (May 2026) skills - these are existing skills found via site search. ClawHub now has 52.7k tools, 180k users, 12M downloads.
